title: nvm_list uses WH_ERROR_NOTFOUND as loop termination, not as error
description: nvm.rs defines WH_ERROR_NOTFOUND=-2104 as a module-local constant and explicitly checks out_rc==WH_ERROR_NOTFOUND before passing out_rc to WolfHsmError::check. This correctly implements the wolfHSM protocol: the server returns NOTFOUND in out_rc when the enumeration is exhausted, which is the normal termination condition, not an error. A future contributor might remove the NOTFOUND special-case and let check() handle it (which would return Err), breaking all NVM list enumeration. The constant's module-private visibility and the comment explain why it exists.
